title: rubus pascuorum
intro: Rubus pascuorum, also known as wild raspberry or bramble, is a type of flowering shrub belonging to the rose family, native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus pascuorum include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and act as an antioxidant, which can help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. Rubus pascuorum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or ointment to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
